mission: Determine whether a genuinely relative or singular Abelian inflow extension automatically induces the residual boundary-state datum, thereby compressing the future Paper 35 frontier from two objects to one inside that extension.

theorem_1_relative_inflow_assigns_bounda.status: derived / conditional / scoped within a quantized relative inflow extension
theorem_1_relative_inflow_assigns_bounda.statement: In any quantized topological field theory with boundary, a bulk manifold together with its relative/singular field data determines a state in the Hilbert space attached to the boundary. Applied to a genuine relative or singular Abelian inflow extension on Y with boundary S^2, the datum (A_rel, m_odd) determines a boundary vector Psi_{A_rel,m_odd} and hence a boundary state omega_{A_rel,m_odd}.

formulas.bulk_to_boundary_vector: Z(Y; A_rel, m_odd) = Psi_{A_rel,m_odd} in H_{S^2,q}

theorem_2_induced_state_is_outside_curre.status: derived / conditional / scoped within the extension
theorem_2_induced_state_is_outside_curre.statement: If the relative/singular inflow datum carries nonzero boundary charge q or a localized singular source, then the induced boundary state does not belong to the current neutral tracial ABCK sector. It is a charged or symmetry-refined boundary state, and therefore supplies the missing surface datum automatically.

formulas.localized_singularity: localized A_rel  =>  omega o alpha_sigma != omega for some sigma

theorem_3_future_frontier_compression_wi.statement: Within the genuine relative/singular inflow extension, the residual surface datum is not independent. It is functorially induced by the inflow datum. Therefore the future Paper 35 frontier compresses from the pair {(omega_sb, q), (A_rel, m_odd)} to the single inflow object (A_rel, m_odd), understood as carrying its induced boundary state.

formulas.current_stack_boundary: this does not alter the exhausted present-stack boundary

imports.previous_boundary: Paper 35 is now reduced to two irreducible future objects on the present stack: the surface-state datum (omega_sb, q) and the genuinely relative inflow datum (A_rel, m_odd). No further theorem-grade reduction remains without new charged or CP-odd structure, or a new theorem relating those two categories.

final_boundary.status: derived / conditional / scoped within the relative/singular inflow extension
final_boundary.safe_statement: On the present stack the final Paper 35 frontier remains the disjoint pair {(omega_sb, q), (A_rel, m_odd)}. But inside any genuinely quantized relative or singular inflow extension, the surface datum is induced by the inflow datum, so the future frontier compresses to the single object (A_rel, m_odd).

primary_external_support: Atiyah TQFT axioms: a manifold with boundary determines a boundary state
Freed classical Chern-Simons theory on surfaces with boundary / gluing laws
